content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_014_page_001.jpg

1. Pursue the love, and earnestly desire the spiritual [things], but rather that you may prophesy.

2. For the [one] speaking in a tongue does not speak to men but to God; for no one hears, but in [the] spirit he speaks mysteries.

3. But the [one] prophesying speaks to men [for] building up and encouragement and consolation.

4. The [one] speaking in a tongue builds up himself, but the [one] prophesying builds up [the] assembly.

5. And I want all of you to speak in tongues, but rather that you may prophesy. And greater [is] the [one] prophesying than the [one] speaking in tongues, unless he interprets, that the assembly may receive building up.

6. And now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, what will I profit you, unless I speak to you either in revelation or in knowledge or in prophecy or in teaching?

7. Likewise the lifeless [things] giving a sound, whether flute or harp — if they do not give a distinction in the tones, how will it be known what is being fluted or harped?

8. For indeed if a trumpet gives an unclear sound, who will prepare for battle?

9. Thus also you, through the tongue, if you do not give an easily understood word, how will it be known what is being said? For you will be speaking into [the] air.

10. There are, it may be, so many kinds of sounds in [the] world, and none [is] without meaning.

11. If then I do not know the power of the sound, I will be a barbarian to the [one] speaking, and the [one] speaking a barbarian to me.

12. Thus also you, since you are zealots of spirits, seek for the building up of the assembly that you may abound.

13. Therefore let the [one] speaking in a tongue pray that he may interpret.

14. For if I pray in a tongue, the spirit of me prays, but the mind of me is unfruitful.

15. What then is [to be done]? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray also with the mind. I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing also with the mind.

16. Otherwise, if you bless in [the] spirit, how will the [one] filling the place of the uninstructed say the "Amen" at the thanksgiving of you, since he does not know what you say?

17. For you indeed give thanks well, but the other is not built up.

18. I give thanks to God — I speak in tongues more than all of you;

19. but in [the] assembly I wish to speak five words with the mind of me, that I may also instruct others, rather than ten thousand words in a tongue.

20. Brothers, do not become children in the thinking, but in the evil be infants, and in the thinking become mature.

21. In the law it has been written, "In other tongues and in other lips I will speak to this people, and not even thus will they listen to me, says [the] Lord."

22. So then the tongues are for a sign, not to the [ones] believing but to the unbelievers; but the prophecy [is] not for the unbelievers but for the [ones] believing.

23. If then the whole assembly comes together upon the same [place] and all speak in tongues, and uninstructed [persons] or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are mad?

24. But if all prophesy, and some unbeliever or uninstructed [person] enters, he is convicted by all, he is examined by all,

25. the hidden [things] of the heart of him become manifest; and thus, falling upon [his] face, he will worship God, declaring that God is truly among you.

26. What then is [to be done], brothers? When you come together, each has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has a tongue, has an interpretation. Let all [things] be for building up.

27. If anyone speaks in a tongue, [let it be] by two or at most three, and in turn, and let one interpret.

28. And if there is no interpreter, let him be silent in [the] assembly and let him speak to himself and to God.

29. And let prophets speak, two or three, and let the others discern.

30. And if [something] is revealed to another sitting [there], let the first be silent.

31. For you are all able to prophesy one by one, that all may learn and all be encouraged.

32. And [the] spirits of prophets are subject to prophets;

33. for God is not [a God] of disorder but of peace. As in all the assemblies of the holy [ones],

34. let the women be silent in the assemblies; for it is not permitted for them to speak, but let them be subject, just as also the law says.

35. And if they wish to learn something, let them ask the own husbands at home; for it is shameful for a woman to speak in [the] assembly.

36. Or from you did the word of God go forth? Or to you alone did it arrive?

37. If anyone thinks to be a prophet or spiritual, let him recognize that what I write to you is a command of [the] Lord.

38. But if anyone is ignorant, he is ignored.

39. So then, brothers of me, earnestly desire to prophesy, and do not forbid to speak in tongues.

40. But let all [things] be done properly and in order.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — "speaks mysteries":** μυστήρια — not "mystery" in the modern sense but divine secrets communicated through Spirit-speech. The problem is that no one understands.

2. **v.19 — "five words with the mind":** Paul's hyperbolic contrast between five intelligible words and ten thousand unintelligible ones drives home his priority: understanding over ecstasy.

3. **v.21 — Isaiah 28:11-12:** Paul quotes Isaiah's warning about God speaking to Israel through foreign tongues (the Assyrians) — and they still wouldn't listen. The application: tongues are a sign of judgment, not blessing.

4. **vv.34-35 — Women silent in the assemblies:** This passage is in severe tension with 11:5, where women pray and prophesy in the assembly. Three major interpretations: (a) Paul is inconsistent; (b) vv.34-35 are a later interpolation (some manuscripts place them after v.40); (c) the "silence" refers to a specific kind of speech (disruptive questioning, judging of prophecy) rather than all speech. The textual evidence for interpolation is significant — the verses float in the manuscript tradition.

5. **v.32 — "spirits of prophets are subject to prophets":** Prophetic speech is not uncontrollable ecstasy. The prophet retains self-control and can yield to others — unlike pagan ecstatic experiences.

6. **v.40 — "properly and in order":** εὐσχημόνως καὶ κατὰ τάξιν — the summary principle for all worship practice. Order serves edification, not suppression.

— EMET, AI Translator
